steps.title: Identify what the research promises
steps.content: The passage establishes the problem (herbicides also kill harmless plants nearby) and then introduces the solution: engineered DNA that would allow “exclusively targeting E. esula.” The blank must state the logical consequence of that exclusive targeting.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — making individual E. esula plants more susceptible to existing chemical herbicides.: The engineered DNA is meant to replace herbicides, not to boost their effect on individual plants. ✗
  • Choice B — enhancing the ecological benefits of E. esula in North America.: The text describes only harmful effects of E. esula; there are no ecological benefits to enhance. ✗
  • Choice C — enabling cattle to consume E. esula without becoming sick.: The research is about inhibiting the plant’s reproduction, not changing how cattle react to it. ✗
  • Choice D — reducing invasive E. esula numbers without harming other organisms.: Exclusively targeting E. esula means only that plant is affected, so its numbers fall while other plants and organisms are unharmed—directly solving the problem stated. ✓
D is correct.
